advantages: groupby expresses entity-level summaries directly in pandas without exporting to SQL for every aggregation step.
merge with explicit how= left or inner makes row-preservation semantics visible in code review and reproducible notebooks.
transform enables per-row normalization within groups (e.g. z-score by store) while keeping event-level granularity for sequence models.
Named aggregations and as_index=False produce flat feature tables ready for sklearn without MultiIndex surgery.
disadvantages: Many-to-many merges can silently multiply rows and distort metrics if join keys are not validated for uniqueness.
Global groupby before train/test split is a common leakage path that inflates offline scores until production fails.
Large merges on non-indexed keys can be memory-heavy; careless inner joins drop unmatched rows and shrink training data without obvious errors.
Complex multi-table pipelines in pure pandas become hard to audit compared with explicit SQL or feature-store point-in-time joins.
